54cfdf5b306364a06a008577_11.spoken: Oh, I'm not hungry. Don't think I could eat another bite. But I *do* have a messenger bag and about twenty empty pockets. C'mon. Let's go mingle.

54cfdf5b306364a06a008577_24.spoken: Parties, breaking and entering, blackmail... sounds like a good time to me. Wonder if Neville has an open bar? What's first? I say we go to the party.

54cff281303031f808007fca_32.spoken: they'd better. If they doesn't, it's all of our asses. Okay. We'll go on ahead and smooth the way. Just give us a few minutes' head start.

Boooring. Nothing juicy at all. Just a bunch of bland business-talk.

...You know what *isn't* bland, $(l.name)? Pheasant. Like the roasted one I smelled downstairs. It's probably sitting on Ma's buffet table right now.

*Right now.*
54d00020306364a06a008a84_18.spoken: Boooring. Nothing juicy at all. Just a bunch of bland business-talk....You know what *isn't* bland? Pheasant. Like the roasted one I smelled downstairs. It's probably sitting on Ma's buffet table right now. *Right now.*

You must be tired, too... to be frank, I don't even know how you're still standing.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_110.spoken: Ooh, big words from the greenhorn runner. Look, I know that they knew the risks, Seattle. We all do. That doesn't change the fact that it sucks.

So, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)... you wanna tell me what you're doing in my bedroom? I'm assuming you're not here to admire the view.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_160.spoken: Welcome back, Seattle. What can I do for you?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_183.spoken: Yeah... yeah, I like it. Okay, Seattle... the Revenge it is. Yeah. That feels right.

The first thing that popped into my head. That's a lesson to remember, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)... the first answer that you come up with is almost always the best one you're gonna have, so just roll with it.

Don't second-guess yourself, don't hesitate, just *act.* You'll live longer that way.

...As it turns out, betrayal *hurts,* $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname).

Malvina felt it, and I felt it too. Tsui had been a friend. I'd just risked my life to save his, like, four hours ago. And he'd tricked me into helping him do something unthinkable.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_500.spoken: No, Seattle. Not for no reason. Auntie Cheng and people like her serve a purpose. You get a job from her, and you can trust that she's vetted the client. That’s important. It protects the whole team.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_510.original:

Cad... well, he was dying anyway. But there were at least a few others whom we maybe *could* have saved. And we didn't.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_644.original: ...And now, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname), if you'll excuse me, I'm gonna crash out for the night. Maybe sleep through tomorrow and get up when the sun goes down. That sounds nice right about now.
54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_644.spoken: ...And now, Seattle, if you'll excuse me, I'm gonna crash out for the night. Maybe sleep through tomorrow and get up when the sun goes down. That sounds nice right about now.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_666.spoken: Don't knock it until you've tried it, Seattle. It makes you sound ignorant. Now, me, I *have* tried it, and I can tell you that it's *disgusting.* But that opinion carries authority, because it comes from *experience.*

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_674.spoken: It's been a long day, and you're still in my room. Why are you still in my room? Go away, Seattle. We can talk more later if you want, but right now you're cutting into my beauty rest.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_675.original:

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_675.spoken: Look, Seattle... we've had a lovely chat, and all, but I'd really like some "me" time. Come back later if you want, but for now, give me some space.

Hey, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)... I kinda want to be alone right now. Personal space, and all that.

We can talk later if you want, but for now, kindly get out of my cabin? Too much socializing makes me grumpy.
54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_676.spoken: Hey, Seattle... I kinda want to be alone right now. Personal space, and all that. We can talk later if you want, but for now, kindly get out of my cabin? Too much socializing makes me grumpy.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_678.original:
Hey, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)... I'm not really in the mood to hang out right now. Heavy stuff going down, y'know?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_688.original:
I think we pulled off a damn miracle back there. Not only did we get a creepy-ass close combat expert on our side, we exposed the Elders and got ourselves a nice bonus for being smart.

Not a lot of jobs go that right. I'm feeling better and better about having you in charge, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname).
54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_688.spoken: I think we pulled off a damn miracle back there. Not only did we get a creepy-ass close combat expert on our side, we exposed the Elders and got ourselves a nice bonus for being smart. Not a lot of jobs go that right. I'm feeling better and better about having you in charge, Seattle.

Listen, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)... I'm all for making allies where we can, but do you really think making somebody *better* at being a vampire is a good plan? I know she said she owes us, but come on. I don't know how trustworthy a leech is.

I like that we got paid, but I can't help feeling we've created a monster that's gonna come back to haunt us later.
54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_699.spoken: Listen, Seattle... I'm all for making allies where we can, but do you really think making somebody *better* at being a vampire is a good plan? I think that went pretty well. There aren't a lot of runners who can say they went up against Ares and came out ahead. Sure, maybe the job could have gone a little smoother, but how were we supposed to account for another runner team showing up?

Adaptability, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname) - that's what makes a good runner. Always go with your instincts.
54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_703.spoken: I think that went pretty well. There aren't a lot of runners who can say they went up against Ares and came out ahead. Sure, maybe the job could have gone a little smoother, but how were we supposed to account for another runner team showing up? Adaptability, Seattle - that's what makes a good runner. Always go with your instincts.

Yeah, maybe Rhombus was a jerk with how he gave advice. But she killed his cat, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). Would you let that fly? Because I sure as hell wouldn't.

Whatever. I just hope it was worth the hassle.
54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_718.spoken: Yeah, maybe Rhombus was a jerk with how he gave advice. But she killed his cat, Seattle. Would you let that fly? Because I sure as hell wouldn't. Whatever. I just hope it was worth the hassle.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_719.spoken: I think we're closing in on the end here, Seattle. With the data we got from the Plastic Faced Man, Josephine's number is just about up. Just think, you'll be able to get your dad back. That's gotta be worth something, right?

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_770.original:
