As you go deeper toward the center, it gets a whole hell of a lot worse.
54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_770.spoken: You were in the safest part of the Walled City, Seattle. The Lotus Den is the lap of luxury compared to the rest of the place. As you go deeper toward the center, it gets a whole hell of a lot worse.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_773.original: You really, really haven't. People avoid the Walled City like the plague for a reason, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). Ignoring that fact would be a whole new level of stupid.
54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_773.spoken: You really, really haven't. People avoid the Walled City like the plague for a reason, Seattle. Ignoring that fact would be a whole new level of stupid.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_774.original:
The bottom? What you saw? No, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). When you went into the Lotus Den, you were on the outer perimeter of the Walled City.

What you saw earlier is the *best* that the Walled City of today has to offer. As you go deeper toward the center, it gets a whole hell of a lot worse.
54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_774.spoken: The bottom? What you saw? No, Seattle. When you went into the Lotus Den, you were on the outer perimeter of the Walled City. What you saw earlier is the *best* that the Walled City of today has to offer. As you go deeper toward the center, it gets a whole hell of a lot worse.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_796.spoken: Don't worry, Seattle, we're fine here. But, uh... let's talk about something else, huh? I don't like thinking about the Walled City very much. Too many old fears and bad feelings.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_802.original:
Something dangerous is coming our way, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). Something that shouldn't be here.

It's inching its way through astral space toward our world, and this won't be a safe place to be when it gets here.
54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_802.spoken: Something dangerous is coming our way, Seattle. Something that shouldn't be here. It's inching its way through astral space toward our world, and this won't be a safe place to be when it gets here.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_804.original:
You're stuck with me, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname).

You have my condolences.
54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_804.spoken: You're stuck with me, Seattle. You have my condolences.

54da5dc6306364a88e0035ea_2.original: Keep your eyes open, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). We've gotten this far by being careful - no reason to change that now. I'd like to get home without any extra lead in my body.
54da5dc6306364a88e0035ea_2.spoken: Keep your eyes open, Seattle. We've gotten this far by being careful - no reason to change that now. I'd like to get home without any extra lead in my body.

54da5dc6306364a88e0035ea_20.original:
{{GM}}Gobbet shifts back and forth on her feet, ropes of hair flying as she looks left and right.{{/GM}}

Shit, shit, shit... This isn't my idea of a good time, $(l.name). We're too exposed on this run. Alarms make me nervous.
54da5dc6306364a88e0035ea_20.spoken: Shit, shit, shit... This isn't my idea of a good time. We're too exposed on this run. Alarms make me nervous.

54de7152653937a40b00028b_2.original: Listen, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). We may have Rooster, but these stiffs were prob'ly just a handful of his guards. He could have more downstairs. We can't leave the same way we came in.
54de7152653937a40b00028b_2.spoken: Listen, Seattle. We may have Rooster, but these stiffs were prob'ly just a handful of his guards. He could have more downstairs. We can't leave the same way we came in.

54e26a883063641415004409_49.original:
Oh, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). Sometimes your naivete is so charming I could squeal.

Look at him, he's wearing a flak vest and packing a roomsweeper. This isn't an office manager or a researcher.
54e26a883063641415004409_49.spoken: Oh, Seattle. Sometimes your naivete is so charming I could squeal. Look at him, he's wearing a flak vest and packing a roomsweeper. This isn't an office manager or a researcher.

54e6531030636444140071ff_64.original:
Hey, chip-truth here, $(l.name).

He messes with you or makes threats, you protect your rep. If you can't do that, the city will stop taking you seriously.
54e6531030636444140071ff_64.spoken: Hey, chip-truth here. He messes with you or makes threats, you protect your rep. If you can't do that, the city will stop taking you seriously.

54eb9f4a3430617007000f3f_4.original:
{{GM}}Under $(s.him) breath.{{/GM}}

Pretty hard to not see that guy.
54eb9f4a3430617007000f3f_4.spoken: Pretty hard to not see that guy.

54ecfca8343061e01b00108f_5.spoken: You heard the man. If you know what's good for ya, you'll clap your trap, chicken man. Can this schooner go any slower? I wanna get paid already. There's a cinnamon bun stall in Kai Tak with my name on it.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_117.original:
This isn't Seattle, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). Perceptions about loyalty matter a lot more here - especially loyalty to your own class of people. If we let them get killed, word's gonna get out. Nobody'll trust us, and we won't be getting decent work any more.

Even if you don't care about that, there's no way this frame job will stick if Ares captures them. They'll know it wasn't the Yellow Lotus that was in here.
54ff5820306364a01b004921_117.spoken: This isn't Seattle, Seattle. Perceptions about loyalty matter a lot more here - especially loyalty to your own class of people. If we let them get killed, word's gonna get out. Nobody'll trust us, and we won't be getting decent work any more. Even if you don't care about that, there's no way this frame job will stick if Ares captures them. They'll know it wasn't the Yellow Lotus that was in here.

54ff5820306364a01b004921_128.original:
{{GM}}Gobbet speaks quietly, so that other runner team can't hear.{{/GM}}

We should let them in, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). If we leave them out there, we're signing their death warrant. I'm okay with killing people in self-defense, but not casual murder. Besides, they might come back to haunt us, and I'm already full up on my vengeful ghost quota.
54ff5820306364a01b004921_128.spoken: We should let them in, Seattle. If we leave them out there, we're signing their death warrant. I'm okay with killing people in self-defense, but not casual murder. Besides, they might come back to haunt us, and I'm already full up on my vengeful ghost quota.

5500650e306364980b004921_25.original:
{{GM}}Gobbet freezes, her eyes wild as they search across the lab.{{/GM}}

I don't like this at all, $(l.name)... We're in the middle of a goddamn Ares facility, we're surrounded by Knight-Errant soldiers, and a bomb just went off. We need to cut and run.
5500650e306364980b004921_25.spoken: I don't like this at all... We're in the middle of a goddamn Ares facility, we're surrounded by Knight-Errant soldiers, and a bomb just went off. We need to cut and run.

550b0530306364f8110000d8_19.spoken: Y'know, I bet we could get that prototype working. We only need the GPS unit, Seattle. I don't like the idea of screwing anybody over. But I also don't like the idea of *not* having an awesome laser.

5522d0686266321c19008e8e_34.spoken: Yeah... that makes sense. Look, we aren't going to force our way through here. That means that we need a key, and *that* means that--

55269cb03063648c150026c9_2.spoken: Sweet heaven. I haven't seen anything like that since Auntie Wong tried to stash some credsticks in a devil rat's nest. It takes a lot to turn my stomach... But we sure have a winner today. This is seriously messed up.

55269cb03063648c150026c9_28.spoken: Whoa, really? That's strange. You're right, though. He definitely didn't see it coming.

55269cb03063648c150026c9_33.original:
There's no fear here, $(l.name). No anger, either. Just this kind of... satisfied feeling.

Tong never saw it coming, and whoever did it was professional about it. Which is pretty odd, because nobody's professional about eviscerating a body, as far as I know.
55269cb03063648c150026c9_33.spoken: There's no fear here. No anger, either. Just this kind of... satisfied feeling. Tong never saw it coming, and whoever did it was professional about it. Which is pretty odd, because nobody's professional about eviscerating a body, as far as I know.

552c087f306364900600298c_7.spoken: Look at this. These scratches look like they're from a long bladed weapon like a sword or machete.

552ff2573063647c16000cd4_6.original: Yeah, well... Somebody in this little community knows more than they're telling us. We should play this close to our chest, $(l.name). If we don't, whoever's responsible is probably going to go into hiding. Or cover their tracks better. Or both.
552ff2573063647c16000cd4_6.spoken: Yeah, well... Somebody in this little community knows more than they're telling us. We should play this close to our chest. If we don't, whoever's responsible is probably going to go into hiding. Or cover their tracks better. Or both.

5530352a323431c41a003ddb_25.original:
Come on, $(l.name).

I think we've learned everything from them that we're going to.
5530352a323431c41a003ddb_25.spoken: Come on. I think we've learned everything from them that we're going to.

55355d69306364e4170078bf_3.original:
{{GM}}Gobbet watches you eat, her mouth slowly falling open.{{/GM}}

All right, $(l.name). I know I love food, but you take it to a whole new level.
55355d69306364e4170078bf_3.spoken: All right. I know I love food, but you take it to a whole new level.

5536c5c5323431e810007d45_30.original:
I *know,* $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). I did what I had to do. No regrets.

But I'm pretty fucking angry about what happened afterward.
5536c5c5323431e810007d45_30.spoken: I *know,* Seattle. I did what I had to do. No regrets. But I'm pretty fucking angry about what happened afterward.

5536c5c5323431e810007d45_36.original:
It's all right, Izz. $+(l.he)'s right - it *was* a bad idea. One that Malvina's to blame for.

I gave her the damned thing to fight Tsui. I didn't tell her to keep it.
5536c5c5323431e810007d45_36.spoken: It's all right, Izz. they's right - it *was* a bad idea. One that Malvina's to blame for. I gave her the damned thing to fight Tsui. I didn't tell her to keep it.

5536c5c5323431e810007d45_64.original:
{{GM}}Her eyes narrow.{{/GM}}

Have you forgotten the first lesson I taught you, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)? "Always go with your gut." And my gut says--
5536c5c5323431e810007d45_64.spoken: Have you forgotten the first lesson I taught you, Seattle? "Always go with your gut." And my gut says--

553835c33234318c0f0043ab_13.original:
{{GM}}Tapping $(s.his) foot impatiently.{{/GM}}

Right, well we need to get going so come on back as soon as your ready.
553835c33234318c0f0043ab_13.spoken: Right, well we need to get going so come on back as soon as your ready.

553959833234319418000283_2.spoken: There y'go, Seattle. You know what to do. Let's hope that Yaz knows her stuff.

553959833234319418000283_12.spoken: Well. That was easy! Go on, Seattle... you take the lead. You've earned it.

5543c201323431601600390f_12.spoken: I... I don't know *what* it is! It talks with Malvina's voice, but it ISN'T HER! If you want to live, you have to do what it says!

5543c201323431601600390f_16.original:
Heyya, Izz. Hey $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname).

I don't remember inviting you on this run, but I guess that I'm glad you could make it.
5543c201323431601600390f_16.spoken: Heyya, Izz. Hey Seattle. I don't remember inviting you on this run, but I guess that I'm glad you could make it.

5543c201323431601600390f_28.original:
I didn't come back here to fight, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname).

This is a recon mission. I wanted to scope the place out, and it's easier to do that solo than it would've been dragging you two along.
5543c201323431601600390f_28.spoken: I didn't come back here to fight, Seattle. This is a recon mission. I wanted to scope the place out, and it's easier to do that solo than it would've been dragging you two along.

5543c201323431601600390f_32.original:
The Sinking Ship is doomed, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). I could feel that the second I set foot on board.

{{GM}}She checks herself.{{/GM}}

Earlier than that, even.

On some level, I think that I could feel it back home. That's why I couldn't stop thinking about this place.
5543c201323431601600390f_32.spoken: The Sinking Ship is doomed, Seattle. I could feel that the second I set foot on board. Earlier than that, even. On some level, I think that I could feel it back home. That's why I couldn't stop thinking about this place.

5543c201323431601600390f_46.original:
You wanna leave? Go the way that you came. I'll finish up here and meet you back on the $(story.Global_HK_Hub_SafeBoatName).

I'm not going anywhere until I sink this thing, and that means finding Cadmus. You can either help me with that or get out of my way.
5543c201323431601600390f_46.spoken: You wanna leave? Go the way that you came. I'll finish up here and meet you back on the Bolthole. I'm not going anywhere until I sink this thing, and that means finding Cadmus. You can either help me with that or get out of my way.

55496bb3303031a8140055ca_106.spoken: I guess this is our new crew. Welcome to the shadows.

554a769e3063642017006b1f_8.original:
Hold your horses, $(string.Global_Gobbet_Nickname).

We still need to plant the visitor record data and find out which floor the lab is on. Looks like we can do that from down here, though.
554a769e3063642017006b1f_8.spoken: Hold your horses, Seattle. We still need to plant the visitor record data and find out which floor the lab is on. Looks like we can do that from down here, though.

554ab102323431c80d0030a0_8.original:
After seeing this, I don't think that this raft is worth saving.

We should go back to my original plan, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). Let's send this thing to the bottom - that's where it belongs.
554ab102323431c80d0030a0_8.spoken: After seeing this, I don't think that this raft is worth saving. We should go back to my original plan, Seattle. Let's send this thing to the bottom - that's where it belongs.

554ab102323431c80d0030a0_25.original:
{{GM}}She shakes her head obstinately.{{/GM}}

We agreed on what needs to be done, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). Don't back out on me now.
554ab102323431c80d0030a0_25.spoken: We agreed on what needs to be done, Seattle. Don't back out on me now.

554ab102323431c80d0030a0_27.spoken: I think it's a stupid idea, Seattle. But if you really want to do this, then I'll help you get it done. At least this way I'll get to look the Rat King in the eye when I kill it.

554bf266323431bc12000f41_2.original:
{{GM}}Gobbet leans in to inspect the terminal. She lifts a patch of exposed wiring with a fingernail.{{/GM}}

Reminds me of your computer back on the $(story.Global_HK_Hub_SafeBoatName), Izz.
554bf266323431bc12000f41_2.spoken: Reminds me of your computer back on the Bolthole, Izz.

554c1fca323431bc1200143f_2.original:
Once we go in there, we're going to be in this. There's no changing our minds after we've picked a fight with that thing.

Whaddaya say, $(l.name)? Are we doing this, or not?
554c1fca323431bc1200143f_2.spoken: Once we go in there, we're going to be in this. There's no changing our minds after we've picked a fight with that thing. Whaddaya say? Are we doing this, or not?

554d1cae323431b417000fc2_2.original: Uhhh... $(story.Global_Nickname_Gobbet)... did we tell Cadmus and his people to mutiny?
554d1cae323431b417000fc2_2.spoken: Uhhh... Seattle... did we tell Cadmus and his people to mutiny?

554d1cae323431b417000fc2_14.original: ...Uh, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)... did we forget something? I've got this nagging feeling that maybe we were supposed to--
554d1cae323431b417000fc2_14.spoken: ...Uh, Seattle... did we forget something? I've got this nagging feeling that maybe we were supposed to--

554d69dc323431f81800014b_5.spoken: Buy me a little time, Seattle. I'm still getting used to this thing, but if you give me a minute or two, those demon rats will be mine.

554d6bf5323431f81800017e_17.spoken: C'mon, Seattle. C'mon, Izz. Let's bury this thing at sea and go home.

554d6bf5323431f81800017e_18.spoken: Yeah, I guess so. But it's *my* devil-egg, and I can bury it at sea if I want to. C'mon, Seattle. C'mon, Izz. Let's do what we have to do and go home.

554d6bf5323431f81800017e_19.original: C'mon, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). Let's go find Cad and see how the mutiny is goi--
554d6bf5323431f81800017e_19.spoken: C'mon, Seattle. Let's go find Cad and see how the mutiny is goi--

554d6bf5323431f81800017e_28.original: $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). Let's go find Cad and tell him that the whole "we're sinking the raft" thing was a false ala--
554d6bf5323431f81800017e_28.spoken: Seattle. Let's go find Cad and tell him that the whole "we're sinking the raft" thing was a false ala--

554d6bf5323431f81800017e_33.original: $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). Let's go find Cad and tell him what happened. I'm sure that he'll be--
554d6bf5323431f81800017e_33.spoken: Seattle. Let's go find Cad and tell him what happened. I'm sure that he'll be--

555a5164323437080f00276b_77.original:
$+(l.he)'s right. From our perspective, there isn't much difference between one demon-god ruling the Walled City and a hundred.

The place is doomed either way.
555a5164323437080f00276b_77.spoken: they's right. From our perspective, there isn't much difference between one demon-god ruling the Walled City and a hundred. The place is doomed either way.

555a5164323437080f00276b_78.original:
So I've made a decision. If $(l.he) *doesn't* go through with this, *I* will.

You need to go back where you came from, or I'll open the choke on the machine and you can die with us.
555a5164323437080f00276b_78.spoken: So I've made a decision. If they *doesn't* go through with this, *I* will. You need to go back where you came from, or I'll open the choke on the machine and you can die with us.

555a5164323437080f00276b_101.original:
{{GM}}Gobbet's jaw drops open.{{/GM}}

Seriously, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)? *Money?* You expected that to work?!
555a5164323437080f00276b_101.spoken: Seriously, Seattle? *Money?* You expected that to work?!

555a5164323437080f00276b_115.original:
Me, too, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). I've been with Izz for too long to let her try to stop you on her own.

Besides, this is fucking stupid. If you can't see that, you deserve a bullet in the head.
555a5164323437080f00276b_115.spoken: Me, too, Seattle. I've been with Izz for too long to let her try to stop you on her own. Besides, this is fucking stupid. If you can't see that, you deserve a bullet in the head.

555a5164323437080f00276b_124.original:
This is fucking stupid. If you can't see that, you deserve a bullet in the head.

I don't wanna shoot you, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). I really, really don't.
555a5164323437080f00276b_124.spoken: This is fucking stupid. If you can't see that, you deserve a bullet in the head. I don't wanna shoot you, Seattle. I really, really don't.

555a61da323431501900164e_16.spoken: C'mon, Seattle. We've got a filthy slum to save from an evil demon-god. Also, I can't believe that those words just came out of my mouth.

555a61da323431501900164e_22.original:
Clear a path, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). I'll go with Kindly's B-Team and look after Ray.

We'll take him down side streets and stick to the shadows until it's safe for us to regroup.
555a61da323431501900164e_22.spoken: Clear a path, Seattle. I'll go with Kindly's B-Team and look after Ray. We'll take him down side streets and stick to the shadows until it's safe for us to regroup.

555ba1a5323431c01900164e_12.original:
That's a hell of a noble goal for a runner. Don't hear that sort of thing often.

C'mon, $(l.name). We can spare him. Let the man do his work - he'll be helping us out in the long run.
555ba1a5323431c01900164e_12.spoken: That's a hell of a noble goal for a runner. Don't hear that sort of thing often. C'mon. We can spare him. Let the man do his work - he'll be helping us out in the long run.

555e6174323431b40a00673b_33.original:
Um. Shouldn't you be lording it over the Repulse Bay? That was what $(l.name) helped you out for.

I mean, I don't want to be rude, but why are you here...?
555e6174323431b40a00673b_33.spoken: Um. Shouldn't you be lording it over the Repulse Bay? That was what Seattle helped you out for. I mean, I don't want to be rude, but why are you here...?

5564c9c5323431a81f00212a_12.original:
I'd think $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname) here knows *all* about that sort of thing.

{{GM}}She elbows you teasingly.{{/GM}}
5564c9c5323431a81f00212a_12.spoken: I'd think Seattle here knows *all* about that sort of thing.

556c9bfd323431c403004932_17.original: No word yet from the old man, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). Think he hopped a suborbital?
556c9bfd323431c403004932_17.spoken: No word yet from the old man, Seattle. Think he hopped a suborbital?

556d2c843234316c180010b2_23.spoken: Good thinking, Seattle. We might need those later. Actually, I could use one right now.

557630cf323431480d002236_1.original: Hold up, $(l.name). I'm not sure we should leave just yet. There's still more we can do to ruffle the qi here.
557630cf323431480d002236_1.spoken: Hold up. I'm not sure we should leave just yet. There's still more we can do to ruffle the qi here.

55820d1c623666c8260088b7_12.original:
He must be disoriented from the ASIST programming. Looks like we got to him just in time. 

No time to catch up right now, $(l.name). We've got to move.
55820d1c623666c8260088b7_12.spoken: He must be disoriented from the ASIST programming. Looks like we got to him just in time. No time to catch up right now. We've got to move.

5584c4ef623666c42b0054df_14.original:
{{GM}}Gobbet looks around for the recipient of Raymond's scolding.{{/GM}}

Uh, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)? Crazy Uncle Raymond thinks the subway car's his momma.
5584c4ef623666c42b0054df_14.spoken: Uh, Seattle? Crazy Uncle Raymond thinks the subway car's his momma.

5585d3103234310001000138_9.original: We'd better get moving, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). We need to stop this before those things drive us as crazy as he is.
5585d3103234310001000138_9.spoken: We'd better get moving, Seattle. We need to stop this before those things drive us as crazy as he is.

5594b0843234312019001a4a_15.spoken: At this point, the only thing we can do is move forward. Go on, Seattle... let's get to it.

559ec0526236669809002374_52.spoken: Sure, Seattle. No need to know were electricity comes from. You just flip the switch and it works, right? So what does your Fortune Engine do?

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_36.original: You better go talk to Auntie, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname).
55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_36.spoken: You better go talk to Auntie, Seattle.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_38.original: You better go talk to Auntie, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname).
55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_38.spoken: You better go talk to Auntie, Seattle.

55a94da3623666741900929c_55.original:
Yeah, talking about all that's gone on. Everything we've been through together.

{{GM}}She shuffles her feet a bit.{{/GM}}

Sorry about Raymond, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). You too, Duncan.

He was trying to do good with that machine. You two need to remember that.
55a94da3623666741900929c_55.spoken: Yeah, talking about all that's gone on. Everything we've been through together. Sorry about Raymond, Seattle. You too, Duncan. He was trying to do good with that machine. You two need to remember that.

55a94da3623666741900929c_86.original:
{{GM}}Gobbet finishes her soup, lets out a little belch, and pats her stomach, satisfied. She tosses the bowl in the corner for Madness and Folly.{{/GM}}

So, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)... what now?

55b134183234315c1800676f_2.original:
{{GM}}Gobbet's brow furrows.{{/GM}}

Umm, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)... what are you doing?
55b134183234315c1800676f_2.spoken: Umm, Seattle... what are you doing?

55de0b3d626632380d001810_2.original:
You've done a great thing today, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). A great thing.

I salute you.
55de0b3d626632380d001810_2.spoken: You've done a great thing today, Seattle. A great thing. I salute you.

55fc514f626632c008005943_13.original:
Another glorious day at sea, drinking the pirates' ninety-proof desperation beverage and waiting patiently to get back on solid ground.

What's up with you, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)? What brings you to my humble cabin?
55fc514f626632c008005943_13.spoken: Another glorious day at sea, drinking the pirates' ninety-proof desperation beverage and waiting patiently to get back on solid ground. What's up with you, Seattle? What brings you to my humble cabin?

55fc514f626632c008005943_27.spoken: Nicely done, Seattle. Ten points for style. So, what did you want to talk about? Go ahead and tell me, I'm all ears.

55fc514f626632c008005943_46.spoken: Another day in paradise, eh, Seattle? Seriously, though... we should get on that run for Qiu and Lam. The sooner we hit that police station, the sooner we can watch Krait's messy, public downfall. I'm not usually one for spectator sports, but this is one that I want a front-row seat for.

55fc514f626632c008005943_60.original: So, anyway... was there something that you wanted, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)? If so, I'm all ears.
55fc514f626632c008005943_60.spoken: So, anyway... was there something that you wanted, Seattle? If so, I'm all ears.

55fc514f626632c008005943_96.spoken: You ask me, that'd be a mistake. I mean, you're really good at what you do. And also, if you bailed on us, you'd miss out on my impossibly charming company. You don't want that, Seattle. It wouldn't be good for you.

55fc514f626632c008005943_113.original:
{{GM}}Her knees flex as she adjusts to a sudden sway of the deck. A rogue wave. After it passes, she puts her hands on her hips and focuses on you.{{/GM}}

Anyway. What about you, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)? How are you enjoying the pirate life?
55fc514f626632c008005943_113.spoken: Anyway. What about you, Seattle? How are you enjoying the pirate life?

55fc514f626632c008005943_126.original:
Yeah. 'Course I do. That doesn't mean that I'm happy about everything that happened to bring us here.

We were gassed, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). Kidnapped. And we didn't even get any of Pang's chicken wings.

As shadowruns go, that was some bullshit.
55fc514f626632c008005943_126.spoken: Yeah. 'Course I do. That doesn't mean that I'm happy about everything that happened to bring us here. We were gassed, Seattle. Kidnapped. And we didn't even get any of Pang's chicken wings. As shadowruns go, that was some bullshit.

5604464e3063648823004d0d_23.original:
Let's not die. But... what about all those test subjects? They didn't choose this fate - Shiawase made that decision for them.

So should we lock them up, or set them free? Your call, $(l.name).
5604464e3063648823004d0d_23.spoken: Let's not die. But... what about all those test subjects? They didn't choose this fate - Shiawase made that decision for them. So should we lock them up, or set them free? Your call.

5604464e3063648823004d0d_30.spoken: Really, Gaichu? At a time like this? I lost my appetite way back in the cryotank room! We can't forget about the test subjects. They didn't choose this fate - Shiawase made that decision for them. So. Should we lock them up, or set them free? It's your call.

5604464e3063648823004d0d_36.original:
But... what about all those test subjects? They didn't choose this fate - Shiawase made that decision for them.

So should we lock them up, or set them free? Your call, $(l.name).
5604464e3063648823004d0d_36.spoken: But... what about all those test subjects? They didn't choose this fate - Shiawase made that decision for them. So should we lock them up, or set them free? Your call.

5604464e3063648823004d0d_41.original:
But... what about all those test subjects? They didn't choose this fate - Shiawase made that decision for them.

So should we lock them up, or set them free? Your call, $(l.name).
5604464e3063648823004d0d_41.spoken: But... what about all those test subjects? They didn't choose this fate - Shiawase made that decision for them. So should we lock them up, or set them free? Your call.

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_60.spoken: True story. We only knew where you were because we could hear you screaming last night. You okay, Seattle? They didn't hurt you too bad, did they?

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_76.original: Can you shed any more light on this at all, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)? I mean, did whoever you talked to in there let anything else slip, or anything...?
5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_76.spoken: Can you shed any more light on this at all, Seattle? I mean, did whoever you talked to in there let anything else slip, or anything...?

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_86.spoken: Someone's coming this way, Seattle. A woman in a red uniform, walking toward the gate. Get ready, everybody. We're about to have company.

5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_91.original:
They didn't just arrest you people who were actually *on* the shadowrun. They arrested our damned boat, and they took everyone on board along for the ride.

The bastards didn't even give me a warning. They just hooked the $(story.Global_HK_Hub_SafeBoatName) up to a tug, cut away her moorings, and hauled us away from the harbor.
5604a7a9643661740e0013c2_91.spoken: They didn't just arrest you people who were actually *on* the shadowrun. They arrested our damned boat, and they took everyone on board along for the ride. The bastards didn't even give me a warning. They just hooked the Bolthole up to a tug, cut away her moorings, and hauled us away from the harbor.

56098865643661c8160012df_45.spoken: All right. I'll get my gear ready. You go back to Lam and Qiu and tell them whatever they want to hear. I'm gonna get justice for Nightjar, Seattle. And I'm also gonna get justice for what happened to you.

56098865643661c8160012df_92.original:
Good call. We should bring her up to speed on what's going on here, as well.

I mean, she's gotta know that the cops have seized the $(story.Global_HK_Hub_SafeBoatName). She probably watched them tow the boat away. She's already gonna be wondering why that happened, maybe jumping to conclusions. We don't want her to come to the wrong ones.
56098865643661c8160012df_92.spoken: Good call. We should bring her up to speed on what's going on here, as well. I mean, she's gotta know that the cops have seized the Bolthole. She probably watched them tow the boat away. She's already gonna be wondering why that happened, maybe jumping to conclusions. We don't want her to come to the wrong ones.

56098865643661c8160012df_96.original:
Okay. Just, uh... don't be too flippant about it, okay? I mean, she's gotta know that the cops have seized the $(story.Global_HK_Hub_SafeBoatName)... she probably watched them tow the boat away.

She's already gonna be wondering why that happened, maybe jumping to conclusions. We don't want her to come to the wrong ones.
56098865643661c8160012df_96.spoken: Okay. Just, uh... don't be too flippant about it, okay? I mean, she's gotta know that the cops have seized the Bolthole... she probably watched them tow the boat away. She's already gonna be wondering why that happened, maybe jumping to conclusions. We don't want her to come to the wrong ones.

56098865643661c8160012df_118.original:
Forget about the bugs, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). We've got bigger problems.

Look, the big question now is, what do we do? We all know the situation that those cops have put us in. So how do we get ourselves out of it?
56098865643661c8160012df_118.spoken: Forget about the bugs, Seattle. We've got bigger problems. Look, the big question now is, what do we do? We all know the situation that those cops have put us in. So how do we get ourselves out of it?

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_78.original:
Look, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)... it isn't our job to protect the poor idiots that Kindly's extorting. That's *her* job, and she's got a small army to help her do it.

If she really cared about the looting, don't you think she'd have sent some 49ers here to lay down the law?
560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_78.spoken: Look, Seattle... it isn't our job to protect the poor idiots that Kindly's extorting. That's *her* job, and she's got a small army to help her do it. If she really cared about the looting, don't you think she'd have sent some 49ers here to lay down the law?

560dd1eb643661700e0076a1_18.original: Right on your heels, $(l.name). Pang's bar should be just around the corner.
560dd1eb643661700e0076a1_18.spoken: Right on your heels. Pang's bar should be just around the corner.

560dd1eb643661700e0076a1_21.original:
Gee, that sounded ominous.

Y'know, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)... this whole riot is a response to police brutality in Kowloon City. Y'think that maybe we should get off the street before Krait makes that call?
560dd1eb643661700e0076a1_21.spoken: Gee, that sounded ominous. Y'know, Seattle... this whole riot is a response to police brutality in Kowloon City. Y'think that maybe we should get off the street before Krait makes that call?

561444d664366150150022f8_50.original: You heard the $(l.man), Pang! We want chicken parts and an ancient rock, and we want 'em *now.* With extra sauce, and a bucket of lobster rolls, and a whole fistful of napkins! Wet wipes, too, if you have them!
561444d664366150150022f8_50.spoken: You heard the man, Pang! We want chicken parts and an ancient rock, and we want 'em *now.* With extra sauce, and a bucket of lobster rolls, and a whole fistful of napkins! Wet wipes, too, if you have them!

561444d664366150150022f8_54.original:
{{GM}}She glares at you.{{/GM}}

...All right. Fine. Give us the rock, Pang. Otherwise, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname) here will drain all the joy out of your life, too.
561444d664366150150022f8_54.spoken: ...All right. Fine. Give us the rock, Pang. Otherwise, Seattle here will drain all the joy out of your life, too.

56218ac8306364fc18005d11_19.spoken: Uh... Seattle? We went right at that last fork. We're supposed to be going left.

562fbdf96436617803006bc6_34.original:
{{GM}}Gobbet leans in to hiss into your ear.{{/GM}}

Hey, uh, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)...? I don't think that this is the right guy. You can tell because he doesn't know what you're talking about.
562fbdf96436617803006bc6_34.spoken: Hey, uh, Seattle...? I don't think that this is the right guy. You can tell because he doesn't know what you're talking about.

5633e0113063644416006cf6_18.original:
{{GM}}Gobbet picks her teeth with one fingernail, flicking whatever she found to the floor.{{/GM}}

$+(l.he) means that we're on the top floor of one of the most secure buildings in Hong Kong. You think one freelancer and some rank and file Fuchi guards are enough to stop us? They're not paying you enough to take on a crew like us.
5633e0113063644416006cf6_18.spoken: they means that we're on the top floor of one of the most secure buildings in Hong Kong. You think one freelancer and some rank and file Fuchi guards are enough to stop us? They're not paying you enough to take on a crew like us.

563905d43063649c0800054a_13.original:
{{GM}}As the sound of the gas explosion fades, you hear the tromp of boots in the distance. Gobbet looks over toward you, his lips pursed.{{/GM}}

Well, I have to hand it to you, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname) - I've started fires, but never just to see what happened. I'll lay good money that almost every guard on this floor is headed to that kitchen now, though. Maybe we can get downstairs unnoticed.
563905d43063649c0800054a_13.spoken: Well, I have to hand it to you, Seattle - I've started fires, but never just to see what happened. I'll lay good money that almost every guard on this floor is headed to that kitchen now, though. Maybe we can get downstairs unnoticed.

563a7eb8306364fc140006b9_88.spoken: Uh-oh. Now you've got them riled up. Seriously, lady, don't push them - this man is merciless. You're pretty lucky we're all *friends* here.

5640cca7306364b811003b46_39.original:
{{GM}}Gobbet holds out her hand, preventing you from moving. Looks you dead in the eye.{{/GM}}

Hold up, $(l.name). If we find a flat like that... we're taking it.
5640cca7306364b811003b46_39.spoken: Hold up. If we find a flat like that... we're taking it.

5643bf5f643661c0130063f0_8.original:
Sounds like a plan to me.

Lead on, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). I'll be right behind you.
5643bf5f643661c0130063f0_8.spoken: Sounds like a plan to me. Lead on, Seattle. I'll be right behind you.

5643bf5f643661c0130063f0_30.original: Lead on, $(l.firstname). I'll be right behind you.
5643bf5f643661c0130063f0_30.spoken: Lead on. I'll be right behind you.

5643bf5f643661c0130063f0_36.original:
Yeah, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)... sure you can.

Lead on, then. I'll be right behind you.
5643bf5f643661c0130063f0_36.spoken: Yeah, Seattle... sure you can. Lead on, then. I'll be right behind you.

565370f6626632d423006c5d_20.original:
There *is* no other terminal. This is it, and it's blank!

We've gotta cut and run, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). And we need to do it *now.*
565370f6626632d423006c5d_20.spoken: There *is* no other terminal. This is it, and it's blank! We've gotta cut and run, Seattle. And we need to do it *now.*

565370f6626632d423006c5d_51.original:
I guess that this is it. Whatever evidence Qiu and Lam are looking for, it's gotta be in there.

Go on, $(l.name). Search for something.
565370f6626632d423006c5d_51.spoken: I guess that this is it. Whatever evidence Qiu and Lam are looking for, it's gotta be in there. Go on. Search for something.

565f332762663208210043d4_13.original: The $(story.Global_HK_Hub_SafeBoatName) is here? But... she isn't seaworthy! How did you--
565f332762663208210043d4_13.spoken: The Bolthole is here? But... she isn't seaworthy! How did you--

565f332762663208210043d4_20.original:
Whatever you say, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname).

C'mon, guys. Let's go survey the damage, see what got broken during transit. Something always does.
565f332762663208210043d4_20.spoken: Whatever you say, Seattle. C'mon, guys. Let's go survey the damage, see what got broken during transit. Something always does.

566f0c4f643661780f0042a2_17.original:
$+(l.he) can't, Duncan. Not if $(l.he) wants the team to survive.

I get where you're coming from, but you're putting yourself ahead of everyone else on this team, and that shit ain't right.
566f0c4f643661780f0042a2_17.spoken: they can't, Duncan. Not if they wants the team to survive. I get where you're coming from, but you're putting yourself ahead of everyone else on this team, and that shit ain't right.

566f17b1643661780f00432b_2.original:
I'm glad that you're doing what Kindly wants, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)... but, I mean, you didn't have to *kill* her.

She could be kind of a bitch, yeah, but...
566f17b1643661780f00432b_2.spoken: I'm glad that you're doing what Kindly wants, Seattle... but, I mean, you didn't have to *kill* her. She could be kind of a bitch, yeah, but...

566f69ef6436613815007c49_9.spoken: I hear you, Seattle, but this is how it had to happen. This is the life that you chose for yourself. You're good at it, and you like it. Duncan doesn't. What did you expect him to do?

566f69ef6436613815007c49_13.original:
{{GM}}Gobbet shifts back and forth, obviously eager to change the subject.{{/GM}}

So, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)... got any big plans for when we hit Seattle?
566f69ef6436613815007c49_13.spoken: So, Seattle... got any big plans for when we hit Seattle?

566f69ef6436613815007c49_18.original:
I don't know about either of you, but I've got a pretty good feeling about this. Whatcha think, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname)? You ready to show us the sights of the UCAS, and get into a whole set of new troubles?

'Cause if you ask me, I think that we're gonna have an interesting life no matter where we wind up.
566f69ef6436613815007c49_18.spoken: I don't know about either of you, but I've got a pretty good feeling about this. Whatcha think, Seattle? You ready to show us the sights of the UCAS, and get into a whole set of new troubles? 'Cause if you ask me, I think that we're gonna have an interesting life no matter where we wind up.

566f69ef6436613815007c49_50.original:
The big orkish drinking hall? Hell, we've heard about that place even in Hong Kong. Habanero fried chicken, racks of barbecued ribs bigger than your torso... 

I am *so* there, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname).
566f69ef6436613815007c49_50.spoken: The big orkish drinking hall? Hell, we've heard about that place even in Hong Kong. Habanero fried chicken, racks of barbecued ribs bigger than your torso... I am *so* there, Seattle.

569596123063640c1f006f01_6.spoken: No. No way. Just *look* at them in there - they're positioned all around the doorway! Look, Seattle, I'm always happy to follow your lead, but this isn't gonna work. Now let's get in there and kill some guys, all right?

56ad342b626632980d007eb8_5.original:
So what are you going to do?

You should at least let us take you back to the Benteng so that you can get your stuff. I mean, it's all still on-board the $(story.Global_HK_Hub_SafeBoatName).
56ad342b626632980d007eb8_5.spoken: So what are you going to do? You should at least let us take you back to the Benteng so that you can get your stuff. I mean, it's all still on-board the Bolthole.

56ad342b626632980d007eb8_11.original: Uh, Jomo... the $(story.Global_HK_Hub_SafeBoatName) isn't seaworthy. She's like a sieve with a rudder.
56ad342b626632980d007eb8_11.spoken: Uh, Jomo... the Bolthole isn't seaworthy. She's like a sieve with a rudder.

56ad342b626632980d007eb8_52.original:
You did have a choice, $(l.name). You could've chosen your adoptive family over your team, but you didn't.

For what it's worth, I'm glad that you chose to stick with us. And if Gun Show has an issue with that, then he can take it up with all of us... we've got your back.
56ad342b626632980d007eb8_52.spoken: You did have a choice. You could've chosen your adoptive family over your team, but you didn't. For what it's worth, I'm glad that you chose to stick with us. And if Gun Show has an issue with that, then he can take it up with all of us... we've got your back.

56ad342b626632980d007eb8_103.original: You did what you felt was right, $(story.Global_Gobbet_Nickname). I may not like what you chose to do, but I get it.
56ad342b626632980d007eb8_103.spoken: You did what you felt was right, Seattle. I may not like what you chose to do, but I get it.

54cfdf5b306364a06a008577_25.original:
Places like this all have one thing in common, $(l.name). The rich bastards who live in 'em always treat the wait staff like crap. I'm thinking that we could use that to our advantage.

Your choice. What should we do first? Sniff around the apartment, or mingle with the idle rich?
54cfdf5b306364a06a008577_25.spoken: Places like this all have one thing in common. The rich bastards who live in 'em always treat the wait staff like crap. I'm thinking that we could use that to our advantage. Your choice. What should we do first? Sniff around the apartment, or mingle with the idle rich?

54d5495c306364bc7300017a_15.original:
{{GM}}Is0bel puts a hand over her mouth, stifling a retch.{{/GM}}

We need to put this thing out of its misery, $(l.name). Nothing deserves to be kept alive in this state.
54d5495c306364bc7300017a_15.spoken: We need to put this thing out of its misery. Nothing deserves to be kept alive in this state.

54d96b813030316814006f66_13.original:
{{GM}}She beams at you. For the first time since you recovered her old memories, she looks genuinely happy.{{/GM}}

Yeah, $(l.name). You, too.

54d96b813030316814006f66_26.spoken: Yeah. A bit. You can probably use the rest, too. G'night, and rest well. You've earned it.

54d96b813030316814006f66_36.spoken: Just... leave me alone. I'm not in the mood to celebrate. All that I want to do is sleep.

54d96b813030316814006f66_50.spoken: Not particularly. I don't remember much, anyway... I've gotten good at forgetting things related to that place. I'm not interested in talking about my dreams. I just want to work on my computer in peace.

54d96b813030316814006f66_62.spoken: Got a couple years to sit here and learn? I can give you tips here and there. But really learning how to do this takes time that we don't have. Time, and desperation, and a need to make do with what you've got.

54d96b813030316814006f66_97.original:
I'm surprised.

You're awfully good at killing for a $(l.man) who wants to live an ordinary life.
54d96b813030316814006f66_97.spoken: I'm surprised. You're awfully good at killing for a man who wants to live an ordinary life.

54d96b813030316814006f66_137.spoken: ...But I'll tell you what. I'm gonna make you a deal. You've pulled me away from what I was doing, so I'll satisfy your curiosity. And in exchange, you need to satisfy mine.

54d96b813030316814006f66_162.spoken: Y'know... I've been thinking about the question you asked me a little while back. The one about the Walled City. The, ah... the one that I dodged.

54d96b813030316814006f66_198.original:
It's all superstitious drivel, $(l.name). The misery in the Walled City isn't the fault demons or devils. *We* created it, and we perpetuate it.

We blamed made-up monsters for our own failings. There's nothing more pathetic than that.
54d96b813030316814006f66_198.spoken: It's all superstitious drivel. The misery in the Walled City isn't the fault demons or devils. *We* created it, and we perpetuate it. We blamed made-up monsters for our own failings. There's nothing more pathetic than that.

54d96b813030316814006f66_217.original:
I'm sure that the briefing I sent you will satisfy your curiosity.

Trust me, $(l.name) - this is a good job with a solid plan behind it. You'll see as soon as you've read it... and speaking of which, you should go do that now.
54d96b813030316814006f66_217.spoken: I'm sure that the briefing I sent you will satisfy your curiosity. Trust me - this is a good job with a solid plan behind it. You'll see as soon as you've read it... and speaking of which, you should go do that now.

54d96b813030316814006f66_325.original:
Gee, $(l.name). I hadn't thought of that. Here I've been, trying *really* hard to wallow in misery and then wondering why I've been so sad!

Of course I can't "just not think about it." If I could do that, don't you think that I would have?
54d96b813030316814006f66_325.spoken: Gee. I hadn't thought of that. Here I've been, trying *really* hard to wallow in misery and then wondering why I've been so sad! Of course I can't "just not think about it." If I could do that, don't you think that I would have?

54d96b813030316814006f66_329.original:
$(l.name).

I've come to a decision. I want these memories out of my head.

They're an unpleasant distraction, and I was happier without them.
54d96b813030316814006f66_329.spoken: Hey. I've come to a decision. I want these memories out of my head. They're an unpleasant distraction, and I was happier without them.

54d96b813030316814006f66_346.original:
Please, $(l.name). You're crowding me. I can only handle so much one-on-one time in a sitting.

Come back later if you want, but for now... give me some space.
54d96b813030316814006f66_346.spoken: Please. You're crowding me. I can only handle so much one-on-one time in a sitting. Come back later if you want, but for now... give me some space.

54d96b813030316814006f66_347.original: I need some time to myself, $(l.name). We've talked enough for now, anyway.
54d96b813030316814006f66_347.spoken: I need some time to myself. We've talked enough for now, anyway.

54d96b813030316814006f66_394.original:
If it did, do you think that anyone would care?

Nobody gives a damn about what's happening inside the Walled City, $(l.name). So why bother spreading the news?

It was our problem, so we did our best to live with it.
54d96b813030316814006f66_394.spoken: If it did, do you think that anyone would care? Nobody gives a damn about what's happening inside the Walled City. So why bother spreading the news? It was our problem, so we did our best to live with it.

54d96b813030316814006f66_404.spoken: ...And that's enough of that. Ask me something else. I don't wanna talk about this anymore.

54d96b813030316814006f66_442.spoken: ...And you want me to answer them for you. All right. Maybe I can satisfy your curiosity.

54d96b813030316814006f66_502.original:
We're close, $(l.name) - we're so close I can taste it. A few more hours - maybe a day or two - and you'll have your father back. Josephine Tsang is going to get what's coming to her. For you, me, and especially for Nightjar, Gutshot, and Carter.

And we've got the Plastic-Faced Man to thank for it.
54d96b813030316814006f66_502.spoken: We're close - we're so close I can taste it. A few more hours - maybe a day or two - and you'll have your father back. Josephine Tsang is going to get what's coming to her. For you, me, and especially for Nightjar, Gutshot, and Carter. And we've got the Plastic-Faced Man to thank for it.

54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_34.original:
She's gone, $(l.name). I don't know where she ran off to, but she looked upset.

She just said something about friends in trouble, then left. By the time I could disconnect from the Octopus, she was on her way out of town.
54d96c4e3030316814006f9b_34.spoken: She's gone. I don't know where she ran off to, but she looked upset. She just said something about friends in trouble, then left. By the time I could disconnect from the Octopus, she was on her way out of town.

54da5dae306364a88e0035e1_11.original:
I don't like this one bit, $(l.name). Last minute plan changes are the fastest way to get killed, especially if somebody bloodthirsty like the Seoupa Rings are involved. 

This whole thing stinks.
54da5dae306364a88e0035e1_11.spoken: I don't like this one bit. Last minute plan changes are the fastest way to get killed, especially if somebody bloodthirsty like the Seoupa Rings are involved. This whole thing stinks.

54da5dc6306364a88e0035ea_6.original:
{{GM}}Is0bel hefts her rifle and nods.{{/GM}}

You're the boss, $(l.name). Lead the way.
54da5dc6306364a88e0035ea_6.spoken: You're the boss. Lead the way.

54dbef30653937cc15006dd1_19.original: What do you say, $(l.name). A little sabotage goes a long way.
54dbef30653937cc15006dd1_19.spoken: What do you say. A little sabotage goes a long way.

54e6531030636444140071ff_9.original: This is bullshit, $(l.name). We've got someone sighting down our backs, and we're exposed out here. If we're not incredibly careful - or lucky - we're gonna be in a deep shit before morning.
54e6531030636444140071ff_9.spoken: This is bullshit. We've got someone sighting down our backs, and we're exposed out here. If we're not incredibly careful - or lucky - we're gonna be in a deep shit before morning.

54e6531030636444140071ff_10.original:
Really? That's what you care about? Come on, $(l.name) - get your head in the game. 

He's a client, and that's all he is. If we don't look out for ourselves first and foremost, we're going to be pushing up daisies come morning.
54e6531030636444140071ff_10.spoken: Really? That's what you care about? Come on - get your head in the game. He's a client, and that's all he is. If we don't look out for ourselves first and foremost, we're going to be pushing up daisies come morning.

54e6531030636444140071ff_28.original:
I have a bad feeling about this, $(l.name). As much as I hate to admit it, Hwang was making a lot of sense when he was talking about Tigath.

Why change the venue at the last minute? This stinks, and it stinks more because I know Hwang's gonna hit us tonight.
54e6531030636444140071ff_28.spoken: I have a bad feeling about this. As much as I hate to admit it, Hwang was making a lot of sense when he was talking about Tigath. Why change the venue at the last minute? This stinks, and it stinks more because I know Hwang's gonna hit us tonight.

54e6531030636444140071ff_60.original:
{{GM}}Is0bel taps you on the side.{{/GM}}

Just a second, $(l.name). Before we go any further... I think it's good that Hwang's shown you the respect you've earned. But don't think for a minute he wouldn't betray you if he stood to gain from it.
54e6531030636444140071ff_60.spoken: Just a second. Before we go any further... I think it's good that Hwang's shown you the respect you've earned. But don't think for a minute he wouldn't betray you if he stood to gain from it.

54e6531030636444140071ff_62.original:
Shit, $(l.name)... Double-crosses are part of the business. On the streets, we have to watch out for our rep, but the Johnsons?

We're all just meat in the grinder to them.
54e6531030636444140071ff_62.spoken: Shit... Double-crosses are part of the business. On the streets, we have to watch out for our rep, but the Johnsons? We're all just meat in the grinder to them.

54e6531030636444140071ff_78.original: This is bullshit, $(l.name). We've got someone sighting down our backs, and we're exposed out here. If we're not incredibly careful - or lucky - we're gonna be in a deep shit before morning.
54e6531030636444140071ff_78.spoken: This is bullshit. We've got someone sighting down our backs, and we're exposed out here. If we're not incredibly careful - or lucky - we're gonna be in a deep shit before morning.

54e6531030636444140071ff_79.original: Really? REALLY?! That's what you care about? Come on, $(l.name) - get your head in the game! He's a client, and that's all he is. If we don't look out for ourselves first and foremost, we're going to be pushing up daisies come morning.
54e6531030636444140071ff_79.spoken: Really? REALLY?! That's what you care about? Come on - get your head in the game! He's a client, and that's all he is. If we don't look out for ourselves first and foremost, we're going to be pushing up daisies come morning.

550a053a306364fc1b000156_13.original:
{{GM}}Is0bel closes her eyes and takes a deep breath. When she opens them, she casts a tired look toward you.{{/GM}}

I don't care what you want to do, $(l.name), so long as we get home soon. I'm tired of this goddamn run.
550a053a306364fc1b000156_13.spoken: I don't care what you want to do, so long as we get home soon. I'm tired of this goddamn run.

55269cb03063648c150026c9_19.original:
Huh. You're telling me somebody killed him and then just... spread him around a bit? Sliced him open like it was nothing?

I dunno, $(l.name). That doesn't make me feel any better.
55269cb03063648c150026c9_19.spoken: Huh. You're telling me somebody killed him and then just... spread him around a bit? Sliced him open like it was nothing? I dunno. That doesn't make me feel any better.

5526a1423063648c15002707_5.original:
{{GM}}Is0bel studies the screen in detail as letters and numbers scroll by.{{/GM}}

Looks like Tong was cooking some BTLs here. He's hacked the peak controller output cutoffs. The delta levels on sim chips are usually about 4 to 5, 6.5 for Cal Hots. His delta peaks are pushing 12. That's brain-burning territory, $(l.name)... And all the drives and chips those BTLs were stored on are missing.
5526a1423063648c15002707_5.spoken: Looks like Tong was cooking some BTLs here. He's hacked the peak controller output cutoffs. The delta levels on sim chips are usually about 4 to 5, 6.5 for Cal Hots. His delta peaks are pushing 12. That's brain-burning territory... And all the drives and chips those BTLs were stored on are missing.

552c087f306364900600298c_10.spoken: Look at this. These scratches look like they're from a long bladed weapon like a sword or machete.

552ffef63063647c16000e0b_1.spoken: This is a whole world of messed up. You think the killer stashed these here? They're stacked. They've been organized!

5530352a323431c41a003ddb_10.original: That wasn't what $(l.he) meant. Tell us about the devil rats.
5530352a323431c41a003ddb_10.spoken: That wasn't what they meant. Tell us about the devil rats.

553548f0323431f0110041bc_12.original:
I don't like this, $(l.name).

We don't know enough about what else is going on down there.
553548f0323431f0110041bc_12.spoken: I don't like this. We don't know enough about what else is going on down there.

55355d69306364e4170078bf_9.spoken: So we walk in, and you go right for the food and scarf it down? You're strange. Real strange.

55369fa7323437682800451d_17.spoken: This guy's memory is going to get wiped one way or another. I'd start asking questions.

55381b9b3234318c0f004284_12.original:
Seriously, $(l.name). You're not an electrician.

Let's find another way past the door.
55381b9b3234318c0f004284_12.spoken: Seriously. You're not an electrician. Let's find another way past the door.

5543c201323431601600390f_43.original: $+(l.he) isn't second guessing anything. We haven't seen enough to make a decision yet. You may have, but *we* haven't.
5543c201323431601600390f_43.spoken: they aren't second guessing anything. We haven't seen enough to make a decision yet. You may have, but *we* haven't.

555a5164323437080f00276b_49.original:
$+(l.he)'s right. From our perspective, there isn't much difference between one demon-god ruling the Walled City and a hundred.

The place is doomed either way.
555a5164323437080f00276b_49.spoken: they's right. From our perspective, there isn't much difference between one demon-god ruling the Walled City and a hundred. The place is doomed either way.

555a5164323437080f00276b_125.original:
{{GM}}Is0bel raises her slivergun.{{/GM}}

No way, $(l.name). You aren't doing this. I won't let you make life here even worse than it already is. I'll shoot you if I have to.
555a5164323437080f00276b_125.spoken: No way. You aren't doing this. I won't let you make life here even worse than it already is. I'll shoot you if I have to.

555a61da323431501900164e_14.spoken: Be careful. There's more to worry about this deep in the Walled City than a Yama King. Everything in this place either wants you gone or wants you dead. C'mon. Let's get this over with.

555ba1a5323431c01900164e_14.original:
Before this goes any further, I'd like to say that I agree with Dimalanta. Think about it: he's after the corporations. We're constantly being hindered by them. Just look around us at what Tsang Corp's doing to the Walled City!

$(l.name), I think it'd be worth our while to let him go.
555ba1a5323431c01900164e_14.spoken: Before this goes any further, I'd like to say that I agree with Dimalanta. Think about it: he's after the corporations. We're constantly being hindered by them. Just look around us at what Tsang Corp's doing to the Walled City! I think it'd be worth our while to let him go.

5568f77f323431581b00491d_15.original: We're all on the same page, $(l.name). Whether we want to be or not.
5568f77f323431581b00491d_15.spoken: We're all on the same page. Whether we want to be or not.

556c9bfd323431c403004932_24.original: I've been watching the comm, $(l.name). Nothing. Think the old man's brain short-circuited?
556c9bfd323431c403004932_24.spoken: I've been watching the comm. Nothing. Think the old man's brain short-circuited?

556cf8753234312c01003971_19.original:
Good. Oh, and $(l.name)? We aren't going loud yet, got it?

Whatever you do, don't start shooting. We can't afford to send Rhombus running before we can get him cornered.
556cf8753234312c01003971_19.spoken: Good. Oh, and hey? We aren't going loud yet, got it? Whatever you do, don't start shooting. We can't afford to send Rhombus running before we can get him cornered.

556cf8753234312c01003971_21.original:
Don't say that. It's bad luck.

Oh, and $(l.name)? We aren't going loud yet - you got that? Whatever you do, don't start shooting. We can't afford to send Rhombus running before we can get him cornered.
556cf8753234312c01003971_21.spoken: Don't say that. It's bad luck. Oh, and hey? We aren't going loud yet - you got that? Whatever you do, don't start shooting. We can't afford to send Rhombus running before we can get him cornered.

556d09653234312c01003b35_11.spoken: Oh, thank you! This is the happiest day of my life!

556d2c843234316c180010b2_15.original:
I don't need cash. I need someone to help me out.

You look like the kind of $(l.guy) who could do that, so just hear me out. This'll take you five minutes, tops, and then you'll have your uniform free of charge.
556d2c843234316c180010b2_15.spoken: I don't need cash. I need someone to help me out. You look like the kind of person who could do that, so just hear me out. This'll take you five minutes, tops, and then you'll have your uniform free of charge.

556e2a46323431bc19001ad9_11.original:
{{GM}}He snickers.{{/GM}}

I could say the same about you. Where's your deck, $(l.man)?
556e2a46323431bc19001ad9_11.spoken: I could say the same about you. Where's your deck, man?

556f6b96323431a812000f9b_3.original: Testing, testing, one-two-three... can you hear me, $(l.name)?
556f6b96323431a812000f9b_3.spoken: Testing, testing, one-two-three... can you hear me?

556fcecb3234319c1b003072_40.original:
What happens to most plans. It gave way to improvisation.

Now, do you think that I can maybe get a hand here, $(scene.Global_PCName)? I give them about five seconds before--
556fcecb3234319c1b003072_40.spoken: What happens to most plans. It gave way to improvisation. Now, do you think that I can maybe get a hand here? I give them about five seconds before--

556fcecb3234319c1b003072_42.original:
Yeah. No way around it.

Now, do you think that I can maybe get a hand here, $(scene.Global_PCName)? I give them about five seconds before--
556fcecb3234319c1b003072_42.spoken: Yeah. No way around it. Now, do you think that I can maybe get a hand here? I give them about five seconds before--

55710d893234316c1c00457a_1.original:
{{GM}}A few minutes later, she snaps the case back shut.{{/GM}}

Thank you, $(l.name). In a day full of unpleasant surprises, this was a really nice one.
55710d893234316c1c00457a_1.spoken: Thank you. In a day full of unpleasant surprises, this was a really nice one.

557205f33234318812001ad5_0.original:
What a damned nightmare this is turning out to be.

I'm sorry, $(l.name), but I couldn't get you your VIP access.
557205f33234318812001ad5_0.spoken: What a damned nightmare this is turning out to be. I'm sorry, but I couldn't get you your VIP access.

557205f33234318812001ad5_6.original:
Oh, and I, uh... I may need your help if I run into more people up here, $(l.name). In case you hadn't guessed, I'm not exactly a social butterfly.

And if things go loud, I'll *really* need your help.
557205f33234318812001ad5_6.spoken: Oh, and I, uh... I may need your help if I run into more people up here. In case you hadn't guessed, I'm not exactly a social butterfly. And if things go loud, I'll *really* need your help.

557650c5623666c02b003595_7.original:
This is the $(l.man) I told you about.

$+(l.he) needs the software that you developed to inhibit your cortical implant.
557650c5623666c02b003595_7.spoken: This is the man I told you about. they needs the software that you developed to inhibit your cortical implant.

557650c5623666c02b003595_32.original:
I'll have Rhombus modify a taser with Dreamland's cortical inhibitor software, $(l.name). The jolt should stop the Plastic-Faced Man's memory wipe process before it starts.

You'll be able to go to the information broker when you're ready.
557650c5623666c02b003595_32.spoken: I'll have Rhombus modify a taser with Dreamland's cortical inhibitor software. The jolt should stop the Plastic-Faced Man's memory wipe process before it starts. You'll be able to go to the information broker when you're ready.

5576771c623666c02b003bfa_1.original: Okay, this is the right place, $(l.name). Emilie should be in the first apartment.
5576771c623666c02b003bfa_1.spoken: Okay, this is the right place. Emilie should be in the first apartment.

557f44ae323431e8180054fd_9.original:
That was one of her creatures. Qian Ya's.

They're all a part of her. At least, they were in the stories. They're going to be waiting for us, $(l.name).
557f44ae323431e8180054fd_9.spoken: That was one of her creatures. Qian Ya's. They're all a part of her. At least, they were in the stories. They're going to be waiting for us.

55820d1c623666c8260088b7_8.original:
He must be disoriented from the ASIST programming. Looks like we got to him just in time. 

No time to catch up right now, $(l.name). We've got to move.
55820d1c623666c8260088b7_8.spoken: He must be disoriented from the ASIST programming. Looks like we got to him just in time. No time to catch up right now. We've got to move.

5585d3103234310001000138_11.original: We'd better get moving, $(l.name). We need to stop this before those things drive us as crazy as he is.
5585d3103234310001000138_11.spoken: We'd better get moving. We need to stop this before those things drive us as crazy as he is.

558898d6623666a012005538_13.original: I'll drop a marker in the center of the Walled City on everyone's PDA, $(l.name). We should be able to weave our way through it to the machine.
558898d6623666a012005538_13.spoken: I'll drop a marker in the center of the Walled City on everyone's PDA. We should be able to weave our way through it to the machine.

5594b0843234312019001a4a_16.original:
{{GM}}Is0bel sighs, her frustration plain.{{/GM}}

At this point, the only thing we can do is move forward. Lead the way, $(l.name).
5594b0843234312019001a4a_16.spoken: At this point, the only thing we can do is move forward. Lead the way.

559ec0526236669809002374_111.original:
I don't know if I can do this. I don't know if I can go back in there.

{{GM}}The little decker's eyes open wide - more white than iris. Her breathing becomes faster, shallower.{{/GM}}

It's not just a thing in there, $(l.name). It's a Yama King. 

A demon goddess.
559ec0526236669809002374_111.spoken: I don't know if I can do this. I don't know if I can go back in there. It's not just a thing in there. It's a Yama King. A demon goddess.

559ec0526236669809002374_118.spoken: It's not just a thing. It's a Yama King. A demon goddess. I don't know if I can do this. I don't know if I can go back in there.

559ec0526236669809002374_125.spoken: Gold?! Are you out of your mind? It's not just a *thing* in there. It's a Yama King. A demon goddess.

55a7e9de6236664014000aa4_2.spoken: Not the best time for sightseeing. The place is crawling with badges. Looks like we get to see a show, though. That reporter already has his hooks in someone, hoping for a sound bite.

55a81618323431f01b008037_11.original: It's like I told you before... we're full up, $(l.sir).
55a81618323431f01b008037_11.spoken: It's like I told you before... we're full up.

56002e56306364541f00015b_4.original:
The germ warfare lab is what we need to find. Should be a pretty straight shot from here to there.

Oh, and $(l.name)... places like this aren't known for the forgiving attitudes of their security staff. The guards are probably trained to shoot on sight, so we should plan on shooting them first.
56002e56306364541f00015b_4.spoken: The germ warfare lab is what we need to find. Should be a pretty straight shot from here to there. Oh, and listen... places like this aren't known for the forgiving attitudes of their security staff. The guards are probably trained to shoot on sight, so we should plan on shooting them first.

56004032626632e01500452a_3.spoken: Ready as I'll ever be. Try your best not to die.

56004032626632e01500452a_5.spoken: Yeah. Okay. I can do that.

56004032626632e01500452a_6.original:
{{GM}}The little decker rolls her eyes at your comment, but the corner of her mouth lifts into a smile.{{/GM}}

History would tend to agree. Point taken, $(l.name).
56004032626632e01500452a_6.spoken: History would tend to agree. Point taken.

56004032626632e01500452a_10.original:
You shouldn't trivialize this mission, $(l.name). Every run could be our last - even the small ones. We're *shadowrunners.*

You've got to think cautiously. Always.
56004032626632e01500452a_10.spoken: You shouldn't trivialize this mission. Every run could be our last - even the small ones. We're *shadowrunners.* You've got to think cautiously. Always.

56004032626632e01500452a_14.spoken: Can't talk right now. I'm busy. *Real* busy.

56004032626632e01500452a_43.original:
{{GM}}Is0bel wearily runs a hand through her hair. Her fingers stumble upon the red cable, and she plucks it out with a look of mild disgust. She sighs.{{/GM}}

All right... I've gotta crack down on this, $(l.name). So if you need something from me, tell me what it is now.
56004032626632e01500452a_43.spoken: All right... I've gotta crack down on this. So if you need something from me, tell me what it is now.

56004032626632e01500452a_44.original:
{{GM}}She shoots you a sassy, narrow-eyed look.{{/GM}}

Not *birds.* *A* bird. It must've followed the $(story.Global_HK_Hub_SafeBoatName) here. The damned thing's been stealing from me this whole time!
56004032626632e01500452a_44.spoken: Not *birds.* *A* bird. It must've followed the Bolthole here. The damned thing's been stealing from me this whole time!

56004032626632e01500452a_55.original:
{{GM}}She hits the switch on her torch and it blazes to life in a quick burst before fizzling out a second later. Is0bel shifts her weight impatiently.{{/GM}}

These people really know how to make a person comfortable - bringing the $(story.Global_HK_Hub_SafeBoatName) out here for our convenience.
56004032626632e01500452a_55.spoken: These people really know how to make a person comfortable - bringing the Bolthole out here for our convenience.

56004032626632e01500452a_57.original:
I don't know. My best guess is that some idiot in a tugboat slammed the $(story.Global_HK_Hub_SafeBoatName) into something when he was towing her. Or some other assorted fuckery... I don't know.

All I know for sure is that something jumbled the components inside this panel. I have to make some fixes to get things functioning again.
56004032626632e01500452a_57.spoken: I don't know. My best guess is that some idiot in a tugboat slammed the Bolthole into something when he was towing her. Or some other assorted fuckery... I don't know. All I know for sure is that something jumbled the components inside this panel. I have to make some fixes to get things functioning again.

56004032626632e01500452a_61.original:
{{GM}}You see the muscles in Is0bel's small frame relax. She seems relieved at the change of subject.{{/GM}}

Some idiot in tugboat pilot jacked up my rig when he towed the $(story.Global_HK_Hub_SafeBoatName) here. I don't know how he managed it, but it's the only explanation that comes to mind. He even managed to damage the contents inside this panel. It's gonna be a bitch and a half to fix.
56004032626632e01500452a_61.spoken: Some idiot in tugboat pilot jacked up my rig when he towed the Bolthole here. I don't know how he managed it, but it's the only explanation that comes to mind. He even managed to damage the contents inside this panel. It's gonna be a bitch and a half to fix.

56004032626632e01500452a_66.original:
What the hell happened, $(l.name)? I mean, I *know* what happened, but why now? Why us? Things were just starting to settle. And now we've been dragged into *this.*

{{GM}}She spits the last word out as if it were poison.{{/GM}}
56004032626632e01500452a_66.spoken: What the hell happened? I mean, I *know* what happened, but why now? Why us? Things were just starting to settle. And now we've been dragged into *this.*

56004032626632e01500452a_104.spoken: Of course I do. I'm a tech enthusiast, not a monster. I know that I can have a one-track mind when it comes to this sort of thing, but I can't help that. It's just who I am.

56004032626632e01500452a_106.spoken: Are you joking? The Bolthole is basically a floating trash heap. More rotten wood than salvageable parts. I'm surprised it hasn't sunk with all the abuse it's taken from us. The Benteng is a *real* boat. The two don't even compare.

56004032626632e01500452a_108.original:
The Benteng is important to the Joho-Lowah. That much is obvious.

It's treated the Captain and her crew well. Can't say the same about the $(story.Global_HK_Hub_SafeBoatName)... at least, not yet.

Maybe someday we could fix her up, make her something to be proud of. But for now, she is what she is.
56004032626632e01500452a_108.spoken: The Benteng is important to the Joho-Lowah. That much is obvious. It's treated the Captain and her crew well. Can't say the same about the Bolthole... at least, not yet. Maybe someday we could fix her up, make her something to be proud of. But for now, she is what she is.

56004032626632e01500452a_109.spoken: Sorry if I destroyed your illusions of hearth and home, but the reality is that we're shadowrunners - we don't get to have homes. The Bolthole isn't really *ours,* anyway. We're more like glorified squatters.

56004032626632e01500452a_110.original:
The Benteng, on the other hand, is cared for and maintained. In return, it's treated the Captain and her crew well.

Maybe someday we could fix the $(story.Global_HK_Hub_SafeBoatName) up, make her something to be proud of. But for now, she is what she is.
56004032626632e01500452a_110.spoken: The Benteng, on the other hand, is cared for and maintained. In return, it's treated the Captain and her crew well. Maybe someday we could fix the Bolthole up, make her something to be proud of. But for now, she is what she is.

560069383063649008006eb1_4.spoken: take a took at that. My guess is that they're all in a deep-dream state via Matrix induction - living false lives to keep them docile. They probably have a bunraku-style lockout once they're brought out of it. In short, they're human robots... all of them.

560069383063649008006eb1_10.original:
{{GM}}Is0bel motions toward the Shiawase device in one of the rooms.{{/GM}}

$(l.name), take a took at that. Each pod has one. My guess is that they're jacked into the Matrix and being forced asleep. Like a dream world that shuts down their higher brain functions.
560069383063649008006eb1_10.spoken: take a took at that. Each pod has one. My guess is that they're jacked into the Matrix and being forced asleep. Like a dream world that shuts down their higher brain functions.

560069383063649008006eb1_13.spoken: take a took at that. Each pod has one. My guess is that they're jacked into the Matrix and being forced asleep... like a dream world that shuts down their higher brain functions.

560304c33063643816003264_1.original:
Locked. Shouldn't be a problem, though.

Try the keycard that you picked up, $(l.name). If I remember correctly, it should have the appropriate credentials.
560304c33063643816003264_1.spoken: Locked. Shouldn't be a problem, though. Try the keycard that you picked up. If I remember correctly, it should have the appropriate credentials.

5604464e3063648823004d0d_25.original:
Let's not die. But... what about all those test subjects? They didn't choose this fate - Shiawase made that decision for them.

So should we lock them up, or set them free? Your call, $(l.name).
5604464e3063648823004d0d_25.spoken: Let's not die. But... what about all those test subjects? They didn't choose this fate - Shiawase made that decision for them. So should we lock them up, or set them free? Your call.

5604464e3063648823004d0d_32.original:
Let's not linger. But before we leave, we should consider the test subjects. They didn't choose this fate - Shiawase made that decision for them.

So should we lock them up, or set them free? Your call, $(l.name).
5604464e3063648823004d0d_32.spoken: Let's not linger. But before we leave, we should consider the test subjects. They didn't choose this fate - Shiawase made that decision for them. So should we lock them up, or set them free? Your call.

5604464e3063648823004d0d_38.original:
Almost. What about all those test subjects? They didn't choose this fate - Shiawase made that decision for them.

So should we lock them up, or set them free? Your call, $(l.name).
5604464e3063648823004d0d_38.spoken: Almost. What about all those test subjects? They didn't choose this fate - Shiawase made that decision for them. So should we lock them up, or set them free? Your call.

5604464e3063648823004d0d_43.spoken: Shit. The test subjects. They didn't choose this fate - Shiawase made that decision for them. Should we lock them up, or set them free? Your call.

56098865643661c8160012df_148.original:
So what's the deal, $(l.name)? Who captured us?

Are they really cops, or are we dealing with something else here?
56098865643661c8160012df_148.spoken: So what's the deal? Who captured us? Are they really cops, or are we dealing with something else here?

560c5be2643661ac15004d4e_74.spoken: Protecting storefronts is what Kindly's 49ers are for. We're freelance. Stopping these idiots isn't our job.

560dd1eb643661700e0076a1_4.original:
This riot is a response to police brutality in Kowloon City, $(l.name). Something tells me that Krait's plan to "restore order" isn't gonna be gentle.

We don't wanna be out here when that call comes in.
560dd1eb643661700e0076a1_4.spoken: This riot is a response to police brutality in Kowloon City. Something tells me that Krait's plan to "restore order" isn't gonna be gentle. We don't wanna be out here when that call comes in.

560dd1eb643661700e0076a1_17.original: Right on your heels, $(l.name). Pang's bar should be just around the corner.
560dd1eb643661700e0076a1_17.spoken: Right on your heels. Pang's bar should be just around the corner.

5612c1fd306364d414004eae_12.original:
It's not her fault, $(l.name). I've heard of this before... I think Fizz's been fitted with a neuroimplant. It rewires the brain to react positively to high-stress situations.

People who have these implants don't break under torture or run away from a fight - they enjoy it too much.
5612c1fd306364d414004eae_12.spoken: It's not her fault. I've heard of this before... I think Fizz's been fitted with a neuroimplant.
